text: Every field of science is dependent on one form of abstract Tabular data or another – spreadsheets of information stored in tables of rows and columns. Common examples range from biomedicine, particle physics, economics and even climate science. A basic prediction task in machine learning is filling in the missing values of a label column in a dataset based on the other columns, and is commonly used for applications in biomedical risk modeling, drug discovery, and materials science, among others. Deep learning has advanced the learning from data field dramatically in the last decade and has garnered a plethora of attention from media sources 3–5 . On the other hand, gradient-boosted decision trees have remained the premier learning algorithm for tabular data over the last two decades 6–9 . Here, we show that the Tabular Prior-data Fitted Network (TabPFN) — a new, generative, transformer-based foundation model for tabular data learning — outperforms all prior works on all datasets tested with up to 10k samples by a significant margin, and with much faster training times. An ensemble of the strongest baseline methods could be trained and tuned for 4 hours, yet could be outperformed by the TabPFN in under 3 seconds. We also show that the TabPFN can be fine-tuned for even more performance gains, as well as used for data generation, density estimation, and learning universal representations, therefore it is a learning algorithm that is itself learned across millions of synthetic datasets, and thus provides insight for developing new learning algorithms. The development of the TabPFN for modeling tabular data has huge impacts to numerous fields of science and can facilitate groundbreaking discoveries and crucial high-level decision making.
headline: Fully Human Written
